summary: Small assembly maintenance PR replacing hardcoded SDA2-base math in `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.s` with symbolic `@sda21` references and derived labels. It partially addressed issue #465 by removing explicit absolute addresses such as `_SDA2_BASE_ = 0x804DF9E0` and using labels relative to `lbl_804DE4F0` instead.
what_changed: Changed `lbl_804DE4D0` and `lbl_804DE4E0` from absolute address constants to derived symbolic offsets from `fobj_c_sdata2_start`.
Introduced `.set fobj_c_sdata2_start, lbl_804DE4F0 - 0x20` so nearby SDA2 labels can be expressed without fixed absolute addresses.
Replaced loads using `lbl_804DE4D0-_SDA2_BASE_(r2)` and `lbl_804DE4E0-_SDA2_BASE_(r2)` with `lbl_804DE4D0@sda21(r2)` and `lbl_804DE4E0@sda21(r2)`.
Touched only `HSD_FObjInterpretAnim` assembly references in the provided diff excerpt, with 6 added and 6 deleted lines.
smart_moves: Preserved the original instruction encodings shown in comments while switching source syntax to relocatable symbolic SDA2 addressing.
Used `@sda21(r2)` instead of manually subtracting `_SDA2_BASE_`, which is less brittle and better aligned with decompilation-friendly assembly conventions.
Derived neighboring labels from `lbl_804DE4F0` instead of baking in multiple absolute addresses, reducing future address drift risk.
Kept the change narrow to one assembly file and one class of issue: hardcoded small-data addresses.
decomp_lessons: Prefer symbolic SDA relocations such as `label@sda21(r2)` over hand-written `label-_SDA2_BASE_(r2)` offsets when matching assembly.
If unknown small-data labels are adjacent, deriving them from a known label plus or minus offsets can remove hardcoded addresses while preserving exact layout.
Hardcoded addresses in hand-written assembly are technical debt for decompilation because they make relinking, symbol migration, and object splitting more fragile.
A useful incremental cleanup PR can be very small if it removes absolute-address assumptions without changing generated code.
naming_conventions: Introduced the local assembly symbol name `fobj_c_sdata2_start` to identify the start of the `fobj` C file's `.sdata2` region.
Kept existing autogenerated-style labels such as `lbl_804DE4D0`, `lbl_804DE4E0`, and `lbl_804DE4F0` where no stronger semantic names were evidenced.
Used lowercase subsystem/file prefix `fobj_c_` for the new helper symbol, indicating association with `fobj.c` data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace `lfs f29, lbl_804DE4D0-_SDA2_BASE_(r2)` with `lfs f29, lbl_804DE4D0@sda21(r2)` to express the same SDA2 load symbolically.
Replace `lfd f31, lbl_804DE4E0-_SDA2_BASE_(r2)` with `lfd f31, lbl_804DE4E0@sda21(r2)`.
Use `.set lbl_804DE4D0, fobj_c_sdata2_start` and `.set lbl_804DE4E0, fobj_c_sdata2_start + 0x10` to model local data positions without absolute addresses.
Anchor the derived labels with `.set fobj_c_sdata2_start, lbl_804DE4F0 - 0x20`, leveraging an existing nearby `@sda21` label.
follow_up_queries: Find other assembly files still using `_SDA2_BASE_` arithmetic instead of `@sda21` relocations.
Search for remaining hardcoded absolute addresses in `asm/sysdolphin/baselib`.
Check issue #465 for the broader list of hardcoded address cleanup targets.
Look for similar `_c_sdata2_start` helper symbols to standardize small-data label cleanup.
